Metadata.siteDescription: Network Canvas provides free and open-source software for surveying networks, designed around the needs of both researchers and their participants.
Metadata.getStartedDescription: Choose the right Network Canvas app for designing a protocol or collecting network data.

Hero.headline: Simplifying complex network data collection.
Hero.description: Network Canvas provides free and open-source software for surveying networks, designed around the needs of both researchers and their participants.
Hero.keepScrolling: or keep scrolling to learn more

Tools.heading: A selection of tools to facilitate your research
Tools.introduction: We provide a complete end-to-end workflow for networks research, with apps for survey design and for interviewing. Using these tools, researchers can easily design, capture, and export network data.

architect.description: A browser-based tool for visually designing Network Canvas interviews. Architect allows subject experts to focus on the design and implementation of their study, without needing to learn complex new technology.
architect.screenshotAlt: Architect protocol editor showing an interview design

interviewer.description: A browser-based app for administering Network Canvas interviews in the field. Interviewer provides minimalist, participant-centric interfaces for all the data collection tasks associated with personal network interviewing.
interviewer.screenshotAlt: Interviewer home screen showing available Network Canvas protocols

fresco.description: Bringing Network Canvas interviews to the web! Fresco allows researchers to conduct Network Canvas interviews in a web browser.
fresco.screenshotAlt: Fresco dashboard showing protocol, participant, and interview totals

Video.heading: Learn more
Video.description: Our goal is to build a suite of tools for the research community that is high quality, free, safe for research, and built to last. Watch our project video to learn more about why we created Network Canvas, and visit our <channel>YouTube channel</channel> for more videos.
Video.title: What is Network Canvas?

Principles.heading: Design Principles
Principles.introduction: Underpinning all Network Canvas software is a set of five design principles. These principles are derived from our observations and experiences regarding the problems facing researchers wishing to design and conduct personal networks research.

ontologicalFlexibility.title: Ontological flexibility
ontologicalFlexibility.paragraph1: The software is designed with fundamental ontological flexibility. Simply put, this means that we do not presuppose anything about the nature of your interview.
ontologicalFlexibility.paragraph2: Researchers are free to define the nodes and edges of their interviews, their attributes, the sequence of data collection tasks, as well as the way these tasks are explained to the participant.

interviewerAssisted.title: In-person and interviewer-assisted
interviewerAssisted.paragraph1: We believe that the presence of a trained interviewer who can facilitate the data collection task enables the collection of higher quality data.
interviewerAssisted.paragraph2: The Network Canvas tools are therefore designed on the assumption that interviews occur in the presence of an interviewer, on an interviewer-controlled machine, and with the interviewer having been specifically trained in guiding the interview process.

emphasisOnDesign.title: An emphasis on design
emphasisOnDesign.paragraph1: Our software has been designed from the ground up to be as visually engaging and low burden as possible for participants.
emphasisOnDesign.paragraph2: We take design extremely seriously, and have drawn on HCI literature, as well as papers from the network analysis community, to create an interview experience that is simple for participants to understand, with clear, consistent, and tactile interactions guiding the data collection.

endToEndWorkflow.title: End-to-end workflow
endToEndWorkflow.paragraph1: Simplifying the process of collecting complex structural data requires not just an interview tool, but also a tool for designing these interviews. Together, these tools provide an end-to-end workflow which lowers the costs (both material and technical) of collecting network data.
endToEndWorkflow.paragraph2: Importantly, our tools do not require a high degree of technical knowledge to operate. Rather, they allow researchers to focus on using their subject expertise to design an effective interview experience.

openSourceCommunity.title: Open-source community driven development
openSourceCommunity.paragraph1: Key to sustaining this effort is input and collaboration with the community, both inside and outside of academia. For this reason, the network canvas tools are free to use and completely open-source (licensed under the GPL version 3).
openSourceCommunity.paragraph2: We have implemented a community driven development program, and are eager to encourage feature development or other contributions from any interested third parties.

Grants.heading: Grants Using Network Canvas
Grants.introduction: We are proud to say Network Canvas is being actively used in a number of federally funded grants in the United States, across a diverse set of research contexts, institutions, and funding bodies.

Publications.heading: Recent Publications Using Network Canvas
Publications.introduction: The following are the eight most recent publications utilizing Network Canvas. For guidance on citing Network Canvas, see our <article>documentation article</article>.
Publications.submission: If you would like to feature your publication, please let us know by posting in our community site <thread>thread</thread>.

Team.heading: Core Team
Team.introduction: Our project team comprises individuals across a variety of disciplines and specializations.

Institutions.heading: Institutions
Institutions.paragraph1: The software is being developed by a team of researchers and developers based at Northwestern University and the University of Oxford, as well as several external contracted developers. We are grateful for the <prior>prior</prior> and <ongoing>ongoing</ongoing> funding from the National Institutes of Health that make this work possible.
Institutions.paragraph2: The intellectual property and copyright associated with the software is controlled by a registered not-for-profit, the Complex Data Collective, comprising the core project staff.

documentation.title: Want to learn more?
documentation.body: We have created extensive documentation, covering all aspects of creating, deploying, and managing a study using Network Canvas.

community.title: Looking for help?
community.body: We have also recently launched a community website, where you can ask questions, report bugs, share your work, and get help from other researchers.

keepInTouch.title: Keep in touch
keepInTouch.body: If you would like to stay in touch with the project, and find out about events and releases as soon as they happen, we encourage you to join our mailing list, and to follow us on Twitter. We are considerate email partners, and will only ever use this list for important announcements.

collaboration.title: Want to collaborate?
collaboration.body: If you are interested in a formal academic or consultancy-based collaboration, please see our documentation article on the subject. Unfortunately, we're unable to offer unpaid consultancies due to limited bandwidth of a small team.

metadata.title: Get Started
metadata.description: Choose the right Network Canvas app for designing a protocol or collecting network data.

intro.heading: Choose where to begin
intro.introduction: Start by clicking the card that best describes the stage you are at with your research.

design.label: Design stage
design.title: Design or create an interview protocol
design.accessibleName: Design or create an interview protocol

learn.label: Exploring Network Canvas
learn.title: Learn more about the project
learn.accessibleName: Learn more about the project — opens the documentation in a new tab

design.label: Design stage
design.heading: Design or create a protocol
design.description: Design a new browser-based study in Architect, or continue an existing study in Architect Classic.

collect.label: Data collection stage
collect.heading: Collect data
collect.description: Choose Interviewer, Fresco, or Interviewer Classic based on how and where you plan to collect data.

shared.schemaVersionInfo: Architect Classic and Interviewer Classic support Schema 7 protocols. The browser-based Architect, Interviewer, and Fresco use Schema 8, which includes the latest features. Upgrading a protocol is one-way, so keep the original Schema 7 file if you still need the Classic apps.

architect.description: Design protocols in your browser with nothing to install, using all the latest Network Canvas features.

bestFor.latestFeatures: Designing protocols with all the latest features
bestFor.interviewerOrFresco: Collecting with the new Interviewer or Fresco
bestFor.upgradeClassic: Updating a protocol created in Architect Classic to use the latest features

interviewer.description: Run interviewer-assisted data collection on a device you control, using protocols built with all the latest Network Canvas features.

bestFor.guided: A trained interviewer guiding the participant
bestFor.researcherDevice: Data collection on a researcher-controlled device
bestFor.latestFeatures: Studies using protocols with all the latest features

fresco.description: For participants completing interviews remotely in their own browser, with centralized study and data management. The sandbox is a public demonstration environment, not a place to run a real study.

bestFor.remoteBrowser: Participants completing interviews remotely in their own browser
bestFor.sharedDashboard: Managing and exporting data from a shared dashboard
bestFor.selfHosted: Teams that can deploy and host a Fresco instance

architectClassic.description: Use only when your study must remain compatible with Interviewer Classic and schema 7.

bestFor.classicCompatibility: An existing study that must remain compatible with Interviewer Classic
bestFor.editWithoutMigration: A schema 7 protocol that must continue to be edited without migration

interviewerClassic.description: For established schema 7 studies and offline desktop or tablet workflows. Maintained for compatibility and bug fixes.

bestFor.schema7Study: An established study that depends on schema 7
bestFor.desktopTablet: Preserving an existing desktop or tablet workflow
bestFor.offlineCollection: Studies requiring the older offline collection workflow

metadata.title: Introducing the next generation of Network Canvas apps
metadata.description: Meet the redesigned Architect and Interviewer apps, Fresco 4.0.0, and the new Schema 8 protocol format.

hero.heading: <lead>Introducing the next generation of</lead> <product><brand>Network Canvas</brand> <apps>apps</apps></product>
hero.description: A leap forward in designing, running, and managing Network Canvas interviews. This page covers what's changing, what's new, and what it means for your work.

definitions.pwa: <dfn>Progressive Web Apps</dfn> are specially crafted websites that can be installed on your device and provide a native app-like experience. This means they have an icon you can click to open them, they have their own storage, and they work offline.
definitions.protocolSchema: Protocol Schemas are the technical specifications that define how protocol files are structured and interpreted by the apps. They ensure that protocols are compatible across different versions of the apps and provide a framework for implementing new features and interfaces.

overview.label: What's new
overview.heading: Architect and Interviewer, reimagined for the browser
overview.introduction: We have redesigned Architect and Interviewer as websites that can also be installed as Progressive Web Apps on your device. Together with Fresco 4.0.0, they form a unified, modern platform built on a shared technical foundation — and all three support a new protocol file format, Schema 8.

lowFriction.title: Low friction use
lowFriction.description: Visit <architect>architect.networkcanvas.com</architect> or <interviewer>interviewer.networkcanvas.com</interviewer> on any device, at any time — and use the tools without installing anything.

installation.title: Local app installation
installation.description: Install either tool as an app directly from the browser. It works like a typical app on your device, while you remain in control of your data locally.

updates.title: Streamlined updates
updates.description: The new apps are automatically kept up-to-date with the latest features and fixes, so you can focus on your research rather than managing software versions.

tablet.title: Tablet support restored
tablet.description: The new Interviewer app once again runs on tablet devices (iPad and Android) after several years of no availability due to app store restrictions.

classic.title: What about the original desktop and tablet apps?
classic.description: The original desktop and tablet apps are now named Architect Classic and Interviewer Classic. They remain available for in-progress studies and are fully supported, but are in maintenance mode and will not receive new features.

architect.label: Protocol design
architect.heading: Meet the new Architect — design protocols in the browser
architect.description: There is nothing to install — just open <link>architect.networkcanvas.com</link> and start building. Architect always runs the latest version automatically. Once on the website, install it to your computer as a <pwa>PWA</pwa> for a native-like experience and local control over your data.
architect.screenshotAlt: Architect protocol editor showing the Sample Protocol timeline

bullets.schema8: Builds Schema 8 protocols with all the new features (see below)
bullets.upgrades: Opens and automatically upgrades older Schema 7 protocols
bullets.templates: Includes protocol templates, designed to help you get started quickly and avoid common mistakes

architect.classicNote: Architect Classic remains available for researchers who need to keep using older versions of Interviewer (e.g. 6.6.0). It is in maintenance mode — bug fixes only — and continues to produce Schema 7 protocols.

interviewer.label: Face-to-face interviews
interviewer.heading: Interviewer — conduct interviews anywhere
interviewer.description: Interviewer has also been redesigned for the browser: upload a protocol at <link>interviewer.networkcanvas.com</link> and deploy without installing anything. Install it as a <pwa>PWA</pwa> to support offline workflows — it remains the ideal tool for in-person, interviewer-assisted network studies.
interviewer.screenshotAlt: Interviewer dashboard showing protocol cards and a resume interview action

bullets.security: New advanced security: encrypted data storage, and app authorization for sensitive actions such as exporting data
bullets.schema8: Supports Schema 8 natively, and migrates all existing Schema 7 protocols automatically when you import them
bullets.compatibility: Compatible with all desktop and tablet devices, including iPads and Android tablets

interviewer.classicNote: Interviewer Classic remains available for in-progress studies. Like Architect Classic, it is in maintenance mode only, and does not benefit from new features.

fresco.label: Remote self-administered interviews
fresco.heading: Fresco 4.0.0 — a significant upgrade
fresco.description: The latest version of Fresco is a huge release, bringing several significant new features. Designed to support remote self-administered interviews (but completely compatible with face-to-face interviewing too), Fresco is the suggested choice for studies with large numbers of participants, a large team of researchers, or that require a centralized data management solution.
fresco.upgradeNote: Users of Fresco 3.x should avoid upgrading in the middle of a study. Fresco 4.0.0 cannot be downgraded to 3.x.

multiUser.title: Multi-user access
multiUser.description: Each team member gets their own account, with two-factor authentication, passkey support, and a full audit log of all actions taken in the system.

api.title: Secure data API
api.description: Fresco now provides a secure, read-only API for programmatic access to your study data, enabling real-time reporting, dashboards, and analytics.

selfHosted.title: Fully self-hostable
selfHosted.description: Fresco can now be installed entirely on your own servers, or hosted in your private cloud, giving you full control over your data.

features.label: Schema 8
features.heading: New interfaces and features
features.introduction: This new generation of apps introduces a new <definition>protocol schema version</definition>, Schema 8, which includes several new interview interfaces and features. If your study requires any of these, you'll need to use the new tools. Choose any card to explore the details.

geospatial.name: Geospatial interface
geospatial.summary: Capture alter-level location data by asking participants to select an area on an interactive map.

details.0: Define selectable census tracts, ZIP codes, neighborhoods, or custom study regions with a GeoJSON file.
details.1: Choose which GeoJSON property is saved with each person, so the response is ready to join with spatial data during analysis.
details.2: Optionally add address search and public-transit context. Mapbox services require an internet connection during the interview.

anonymisation.name: Anonymisation interface
anonymisation.summary: Give participants direct control over sensitive identifiers by encrypting selected node or edge attributes on their device.

details.0: Each participant creates the passphrase used to protect their selected data, and that passphrase is never stored by the app.
details.1: Researchers cannot decrypt the protected values; exports replace encrypted attributes with a clear marker instead.
details.2: This is an experimental feature for studies where stronger privacy assurances may support trust and disclosure.

oneToMany.name: One-to-many dyad census interface
oneToMany.summary: Create ties from one focal person to several other people on the same screen instead of considering every pair separately.

details.0: Use it when a network contains central figures who are likely to have relationships with many others.
details.1: Collect one or more relationship types while keeping the focal person visible throughout the task.
details.2: Decide whether each focal person returns to the list for another pass or is removed after they have been considered.

familyPedigree.name: Family pedigree interface
familyPedigree.summary: Guide participants through building a complex family structure in a dedicated visual workflow.

details.0: Create family members and relationships together, with automatic layout keeping the pedigree readable as it grows.
details.1: Represent biological, social, adoptive, donor, surrogate, and partner relationships, and collect attributes for every family member.
details.2: Add an optional disease-nomination step to record which relatives are affected by specific conditions for later visualization.

narrativePedigree.name: Narrative pedigree interface
narrativePedigree.summary: Turn an earlier Family Pedigree into a read-only visualization that uses standard clinical pedigree notation.

details.0: Show affected relatives and definite carriers for one or more conditions across supported inheritance patterns.
details.1: Optionally display possible at-risk statuses when the study is designed for appropriately guided interpretation.
details.2: Focus on one person's contributing lineage and export a printable snapshot. The visualization is an aid, not a diagnosis.

networkComposer.name: Network composer interface
networkComposer.summary: Build and edit a participant's whole personal network on one free-form canvas.

details.0: Add people, complete person and relationship attributes, draw several relationship types, and reposition the network as the conversation develops.
details.1: Use lasso selection, groups, automatic layout, keyboard shortcuts, and undo or redo to construct dense networks efficiently.
details.2: Treat it as a researcher- or interviewer-led network notepad rather than a step-by-step self-administered task.

validation.name: Richer field validation
validation.summary: Express relationships between answers directly in the protocol, so forms can catch inconsistent responses while the interview is still in progress.

details.0: Compare a numeric response with another variable using the new greater-than-variable and less-than-variable rules.
details.1: Combine these relative checks with existing required, range, uniqueness, and cross-field rules to describe the answers your study can accept.
details.2: Preview the form in Architect and test edge cases before deployment; the same protocol rules run in Interviewer and Fresco.

enhancedSkipLogic.name: Enhanced skip logic
enhancedSkipLogic.summary: Build more precise interview routes with expanded comparison rules and explicit destinations for each skipped stage.

details.0: Use “contains” and “does not contain” with text responses, and match several selected values in categorical and ordinal rules.
details.1: Continue at the next available stage, jump forward to a chosen later stage, or route directly to the interview finish screen.
details.2: Keep every destination forward-moving, making complex paths predictable and avoiding loops during data collection.

nodeShapes.name: Configurable node shapes
nodeShapes.summary: Use shape as an additional visual channel, making different kinds of people easier to distinguish in dense network interfaces.

details.0: Choose circles, squares, or diamonds as a default shape for each person type.
details.1: Map shapes dynamically from categorical, ordinal, boolean, number, or scalar variables when the visual distinction carries data.
details.2: Combine shape with color and size so important categories remain legible without depending on a single visual cue.

formHints.name: Form hints and validation guidance
formHints.summary: Place concise guidance beside an individual question, giving participants context exactly where they need it.

details.0: Add a markdown-formatted hint to a form field without lengthening the main stage prompt.
details.1: Use hints to clarify expected formats, unfamiliar terms, or how a response will be interpreted.
details.2: Optionally show automatic guidance derived from the field's validation rules, such as acceptable ranges or required responses.

protocolTemplates.name: Protocol templates
protocolTemplates.summary: Start from a research-grounded protocol instead of an empty timeline, then adapt it to your study.

details.0: Explore templates for transnational, mental-health, social-isolation, behavioral-influence, care-and-support, and sexual or injection-risk networks.
details.1: Use the general Sample Protocol to learn how key Network Canvas features and techniques fit together.
details.2: Edit every stage, prompt, variable, and visual choice; your adapted protocols remain stored locally in your browser.

responsiveSvg.name: Responsive SVG sociogram backgrounds
responsiveSvg.summary: Create sociogram backgrounds whose regions, axes, and labels adapt to both portrait and landscape interview canvases.

details.0: Upload an SVG whose elements are positioned relative to the available canvas instead of a fixed pixel grid.
details.1: Keep labels readable and regions extended to the canvas edges when participants rotate a tablet or use a different screen shape.
details.2: Use the provided social-context template as a starting point for quadrants, independent axes, categories, or overlapping contexts.

syntheticData.name: Synthetic interview data
syntheticData.summary: Generate realistic test interviews before recruitment so you can inspect the complete data workflow without putting participant data at risk.

details.0: Create up to 1,000 synthetic interviews at once and inspect how responses appear in study tables and exports.
details.1: Optionally simulate participant drop-out and respect the protocol's skip logic and network filters.
details.2: Synthetic records are flagged separately and can be removed in bulk without touching real interviews.

encryptionAtRest.name: On-device encryption at rest
encryptionAtRest.summary: Protect protocols, interviews, and settings stored on the device whenever you configure an Interviewer app lock.

details.0: Choose a PIN, passphrase, or supported biometric method during setup; the same authentication unlocks the key used to decrypt local data.
details.1: Keep the decryption key only in memory while Interviewer is unlocked. Reloading, locking, closing, or reaching the idle timeout removes it.
details.2: Use this app-level protection alongside full-device encryption and regular exports as part of a layered data-security plan.

authorisationChecks.name: Configurable authorization checks
authorisationChecks.summary: Require a fresh unlock at sensitive workflow boundaries instead of relying only on the app's general idle lock.

details.0: Independently require re-authentication before entering an interview, leaving it for the dashboard, or exporting collected data.
details.1: Use the already configured PIN, passphrase, or biometric method, without introducing a second set of credentials.
details.2: Keep protocol names, session counts, and stored data hidden whenever Interviewer is locked.

multiUser.name: Multi-user administration
multiUser.summary: Give each researcher a separate administrator account instead of sharing one set of dashboard credentials.

details.0: Add and remove multiple administrator accounts from settings; every current account has equal administrative access.
details.1: Choose password authentication with optional TOTP two-factor protection, or use passkeys for password-free sign-in.
details.2: Attribute dashboard activity to usernames in the shared audit feed, including authentication, protocol, export, and account-management events.

advancedExportFiltering.name: Advanced export filtering
advancedExportFiltering.summary: Find and export precisely the interviews you need, even when a large study spans many pages of results.

details.0: Filter server-side by protocol, date, progress, network size, export status, or participant identifier across the complete dataset.
details.1: Select every interview matching the active filters, not only the rows currently visible on screen.
details.2: Export all, completed, or unexported interviews within the filtered result set while leaving unrelated records untouched.

secureDataApi.name: Secure Interview Data API
secureDataApi.summary: Connect analysis tools directly to a Fresco deployment through a versioned, read-only JSON API.

details.0: Keep the API disabled until it is needed, then create bearer tokens from the authenticated dashboard.
details.1: List and retrieve interview networks and protocol metadata without exposing any endpoint that can modify study data.
details.2: Revoke tokens, review when each token was last used, and build repeatable R, Python, dashboard, or reporting workflows.

compatibility.label: Compatibility
compatibility.heading: What to know before you upgrade
compatibility.introduction: Protocol schema versions determine which apps can open a given protocol file — and the most important change in this release is Schema 8. Below you will find a table that indicates exactly how every app handles both formats. Select a row to see details about what it means in practice.
compatibility.defaultNote: Select any row above to see what it means for your protocol files.

interviewerClassic.note: Interviewer Classic runs Schema 7 natively, but cannot open Schema 8 protocols. Continue using it for in-progress Schema 7 studies — it remains supported with bug fixes.

architectClassic.note: Architect Classic continues to produce Schema 7 protocols, in maintenance mode. Use it only if you need to keep running older versions of Interviewer (e.g. 6.6.0).

frescoClassic.note: Fresco 3.1.2 runs Schema 7 natively but will not open Schema 8 protocols. Upgrade to Fresco 4.0.0 to use the new format.

interviewer.note: The new Interviewer opens Schema 8 natively. Opening a Schema 7 protocol migrates it to Schema 8 automatically — and one way.

architect.note: The new Architect builds Schema 8 protocols, and opens and automatically upgrades older Schema 7 protocols created in Architect Classic.

fresco.note: Fresco 4.0.0 runs Schema 8 natively; uploaded Schema 7 protocols are migrated to Schema 8 automatically — and one way.

caution.label: Caution
caution.heading: Protocol migration is one-way.
caution.description: If you open a Schema 7 protocol in any new-generation app, it will be automatically upgraded to Schema 8. You will not be able to convert it back, or open it in the older apps afterward. This is especially important if you make changes to a protocol in Architect, as it will be upgraded to Schema 8 when you save it.
caution.fileNote: Keep a copy of your original <file>.netcanvas</file> file if you need to continue running it in Interviewer Classic, Architect Classic, or Fresco 3.x.

newStudy.label: Starting a new study?
newStudy.heading: Use the new generation
newStudy.description: For new studies, we recommend the new tools: use Architect to design your protocol, and deploy interviews with Fresco 4.0.0 or Interviewer, depending on your needs.

ongoing.label: Running an ongoing study?
ongoing.heading: There is no rush!
ongoing.description: Interviewer Classic and Architect Classic are still supported and will continue to receive bug fixes. Upgrade at a time that suits your project — just be mindful of the one-way migration when you do.

resources.label: Project resources
resources.heading: Revamped documentation and a new project website
resources.introduction: The apps are not the only part of Network Canvas that has changed. We have also redesigned the project website and reorganized the documentation to make it easier to understand the platform, choose the right tools, and find your next step.

documentation.label: Project documentation
documentation.heading: Guidance organized around your research workflow
documentation.description: The documentation is now structured around the stage of your research that you are at: getting started, designing protocols, collecting data, and analyzing results. New workflow navigation and refreshed guides make the next step easier to find.
documentation.screenshotAlt: The redesigned Network Canvas documentation homepage

website.label: Project website
website.heading: A fresh new look for networkcanvas.com
website.description: The redesigned website introduces a more expressive visual identity and clearer paths through the Network Canvas ecosystem, from learning what the platform can do to choosing the best tools for your study.
website.screenshotAlt: The redesigned Network Canvas website homepage

destinations.label: Getting started
destinations.heading: Start exploring the new generation
destinations.closing: If you have questions or run into issues, the <community>community forum</community> is the best place to reach us and connect with other researchers using the tools. We hope these new tools make a meaningful difference in your research — our team is extremely grateful for feedback from the community, and for the ongoing support that makes this work possible.

architect.title: Open Architect
architect.description: Build, validate, and preview Schema 8 protocols directly in your browser, with a local library that stays on your device.

interviewer.title: Open Interviewer
interviewer.description: Run secure, offline-capable interviews on desktop and tablet, then export completed sessions from the same local app.

fresco.title: Deploy Fresco 4.0.0
fresco.description: Set up the multi-user platform for remote self-administered studies, centralized data management, and research teams.

documentation.title: Explore the docs
documentation.description: Follow task-focused guidance for choosing tools, designing protocols, collecting data, and understanding every interface.
